title: The Afterparty

[[File:PartyCrasherLogo.png|200px|link=]]






After so many years… We've finally found it.

Have you ever wondered what happens to those who succumb to the [[Sanguine Festivus|unspoken disease]]?

Have you ever wondered all where their sanity goes?





|final=|body= {{Collapse|num=2|initial=
[[File:PartyCrasherLogo.png|200px|link=]]





We're not the [[M.E.G.]]

We're not going to keep this information from you.

Read this at your own discretion.

Just know that you have been warned.




|final=|body= {{SFV Theme}}
{{Blurquote|image=Afterparty.png|quote-text=
Welcome to the
AFTER
 |person=Anonymous}}
{{Underline|FFFFFF}} {{Class Custom Image|hexagon=Ψ|bg-image=SFV_green.jpeg|text-color=white|class=Fun|safety=Unsafe|security=Unstable|entity=Mental Hazard}} '''The Afterparty''' is an anomalous sublevel of [[Level Fun]], specifically the [[Level Fun/The Party Rooms|Party Rooms]]. {{Blurimage|file=Afterparty.png|hovertext=Untouched and eternal...|caption=One of the only confirmed images of the Afterparty, likely taken by [[Entity 1|the Cameraman]].}} == Description == '''The Afterparty''' is a series of interconnected rooms containing many party supplies, each resembling a classic children's party venue. Just as mazelike and labyrinthine as [[Level Fun|its parent level]], this sublevel is eerily devoid of any sentient presence due to [[Phenomenon 15|certain phenomena]] which shroud its ever-shifting halls, exuding a strange atmosphere of melancholy loneliness comparable to the innate and silent emptiness of the house after the party has ended and the guests all gone home. Dozens of party hats sit, unmoving, on cold and unused wooden tables; and from the chairs which nobody sits on there are slung colourful helium balloons, floating straight up in the air, never seeming to droop nor lose their gas, like a breath inhaled but not yet released, waiting for hours untold to be exhaled. Though there exist no physical dangers in '''the Afterparty''', it is known that many psychological perils are present here. Live recordings from explorers roaming the level have described hearing human screams emanating from the depths of the winding corridors, starting out soft but eventually rising in intensity until the wanderer can bear it no longer, and is either driven insane or drops dead from the auditory overload. Intriguingly, these shrieks appear to be hallucinations propagated by '''the Afterparty''', as they have never been able to be recorded, only heard by those who are actively present amongst the halls of the forsaken labyrinth. Many who have found a way out of '''the Afterparty''' were reported to have already gone insane, displaying interchangeably the behaviour of both [[Phenomenon 35|wretches]] and [[Sanguine Festivus|partygoers]], which includes the desperate clawing of the skin with no apparent goal and an insatiable hunger than often drives victims of the level to such measures as cannibalisation. The lucky few who have escaped '''the Afterparty''' with their life and their sanity were forever scarred afterwards, notably displaying a similar demeanour as those who have escaped [[Level !]] — though unlike the wanderers who have leapt from the jaws of [[Level !|the exclamation]], there is every chance that escapees of '''the Afterparty''' may stumble back into its clutches, especially in the few months following their egress of the level. == Entities == '''The Afterparty''' is completely devoid of entities. The effects of [[Phenomenon 15]] notwithstanding, researchers have wondered why there are no signs at all of [[Sanguine Festivus|partygoer]] or [[Sanguine Festivus|SFV]] presence within the level, considering it is a sublevel of the [[Level Fun/The Party Rooms|Party Rooms]]. When asked to comment on this detail, [[Caius Taggert|Sir Caius Taggert]], the de facto sovereign of [[Level Fun]], replied that the [[Sanguine Festivus|partygoers]] simply "prefer a more fun place to party at". == Colonies and Outposts == Due to the [[Phenomenon 15|Alone Phenomenon]] and the hazardous nature of '''the Afterparty''', no groups or colonies have been established in this level. {{Blurimage|file=Afterpartyimage3.webp|caption=The earliest known human-taken photograph of '''the Afterparty''', upon entry from what used to be [[Level 434|Level 9.12]].|hovertext=Forgotten memories...}} == Discovery == It is well-known that the pre-[[Phenomenon 12|Flicker]] iteration of [[Level Fun]] was simply a mazelike complex, presumably later transforming into the infamous [[Level Fun/The Party Rooms|Party Rooms]]. Following the events of [[Phenomenon 12|the Flicker]], [[Level Fun]] then managed to evolve into a triad of even more labyrinthine layers, and though the [[Level Fun/The Party Rooms|Party Rooms]] was one of these sectors, it was evident that something had changed — the [[Level Fun/The Party Rooms|Party Rooms]] were far too bright and vividly coloured, nothing like the original duller appearance of the [[Sanguine Festivus|partygoers]]' domain. Then, in 2018, a lone [[Party Crashers|Party Crasher]] stumbled upon a new level. It resembled more than anything what the [[Level Fun/The Party Rooms|Party Rooms]] had been pre-[[Phenomenon 12|Flicker]], and the league decided that they must have stumbled upon a new sublevel of [[Level Fun]]: '''the Afterparty'''. In the years following the level's discovery, researchers deduced that '''the Afterparty''' was [[Phenomenon 100|split]] from [[Level Fun]] during [[Phenomenon 12|the Flicker]], and they theorised that one half of this riven level was warped by the incident into what we know today, whilst the other half was not altered as much — thus becoming '''the Afterparty'''. However, many unanswered questions still remain about the level, such as that of why no [[Sanguine Festivus]] still lingers there, if it was truly created by a [[Phenomenon 100|split]] from its parent level. Additionally, certain hypothesists believe that the entire existence of '''the Afterparty''' has, in fact, something to do with the [[Caius Taggert|Party Host]] and his mind-warping abilities. == Entrances and Exits == === Entrances === The most common fashion in which wanderers are known to enter '''the Afterparty''' is through a ramshackle wooden door in the [[Level Fun/The Party Rooms|Party Rooms]] or the [[Level Fun/The Play Rooms|Play Rooms]]. This door has been described as terribly out-of-place amongst the bright colours of the level, and may also be distinguished by certain marks such as a bloodied smiley face scratched into the decaying wood. However, the following methods of entry to '''the Afterparty''' have also been recorded: * Stepping into an anomalous dark puddle in [[Level 3]] which shows a warped reflection (often including a Glasgow smile) of the wanderer will cause one to clip through it as if it were a [[Level 1|false puddle]], and enter '''the Afterparty'''. * Following sounds of screaming mixed with demented laughter in [[Level 5]] has a chance to lead one to '''the Afterparty'''. * Entering a section of [[Level 38]] where the level's hue is mute green rather than orange will cause one to eventually pass out and wake up in '''the Afterparty'''. * In [[Level 434]], finding a house in which balloons are attached to the furniture will act as a seamless transition to '''the Afterparty'''. === Exits === '''The Afterparty''' is known to have been exited in these various ways: * The halls may sometimes fade into the bouncehouses of [[Level Fun]]. * Opening a heavy, dark blue door apparently composed of marble will lead one to [[Level 37.1]]. * No-clipping through an arcade machine which rarely appears will send one to the Final Arcade of [[Level 399]]. * Breaking through the wall with one's teeth may open a rift to [[Level 610|the Snackrooms]]. This is mostly known to happen when victims of '''the Afterparty''' succumb to the insane hunger bestowed upon them by the level, and attempt to eat the walls in desperation. * Opening a red door fashioned with an exclamation mark will lead one to [[Level !]]. There are several other loosely documented methods of egress, such as one purported by a wanderer who supposedly no-clipped through some pipelines in '''the Afterparty''' and ended up in [[Level 2]]. However, none of these rumours have been officially confirmed. == Addendum ==
[[File:PartyCrasherLogo.png|right|120x120px|link=]] '''Notice:'''
Data retrieval successful. '''Fetching data...'''
{{Fullscreen|number=1|show='''> The Mind's Eclipse <'''|hide=> Back|padding=30px|content= {{Background|file=SFV.gif}}
'''The Mind's Eclipse''' {{Underline|FFFFFF}} ''The following is a partial transcript of a classified recording concerning a valuable piece of information regarding '''the Afterparty''', which comes from the masked mouth of the [[Caius Taggert|Party Host]] himself. This transcription was generously provided to the [[Party Crashers]] by [[The Party Pooper|Thomas Jefferson Wilson]], as part of a larger audio recording of a [[The Co-Hosts|Fun governmental]] meeting he stumbled upon during one of his 2020 expeditions into [[Level Fun]]. Note that this information may not necessarily be true, as [[Caius Taggert|Sir Taggert]] is known for his cunning and deceptive nature.'' {{Underline|FFFFFF}} '''> Begin Log <''' '''Taggert:''' [[Sanguine Festivus]] is unlike any other disease known to mankind, and for good reason too. Do you know, my [[he Co-Hosts|darlin' children]], why you and I can keep some of our wits, but the rest of our kind ain't capable of basic cognitive function at all? '''Euphoria:''' Ahahaha, this is what we've all been waiting for, guys! He's finally gonna tell us— '''Melody:''' What? No! I hate spoilers. No spoilers! Don't tell me anything! '''Taggert:''' You're insufferable. Well, my friends, it has everything to do with '''the Afterparty'''. '''Melody:''' I told you, no spoilers! Why— '''Bacchus:''' Melody, Melody. Chill. Just chill, oka— '''Melody:''' How am I supposed to chill when he just freakin' spoiled the enti— '''Taggert:''' Will you stop your bickerin'?! I ain't tellin' you nothin' if you ain't going to listen. '''Euphoria:''' I'm listening! I'm all ears (even though I'm actually deaf in one ear)! Just don't worry about the rest of them, the— '''Festus:''' Stupid teacher's pet. '''Euphoria:''' What did you just— '''Taggert:''' Quieten down! Quieten down, or I shall have to impale you through the heart with my catchpole! '''Hedone:''' Ooh, that sounds fun! I wanna be impaled through the heart with— '''Taggert:''' Silence! ''(An uncharacteristic quietude follows, presumably caused by [[Caius Taggert|Sir Taggert]] utilising his mind-warping abilities to silence [[The Co-Hosts|his followers]].)'' '''Taggert:''' Now, children. When [[Sanguine Festivus]] infects a wanderer, their soul don't just vanish like that. It leaves 'em, yes, but it don't disappear completely. '''The Mind's Eclipse''', it's called, for your wits are still floatin' about somewhere, just not in your head. Now, I obviously couldn't have free-floatin' souls just cavortin' 'round [[Level Fun]] twenty-four/seven, so— '''Festus:''' Did you just say ''six seven''? '''Taggert:''' —''so'', I decided to take it upon meself to bottl'up all these souls into my own brain. Unfortunately, despite my big brain— '''Bacchus:''' Did he just say ''big brain''? '''Taggert:''' Ahem! Will you listen! '''Bacchus:''' I'm hard of hearing, Caius! Side effect of, y'know, Euphoria eating my eardrums out. All that blood... eugh. I will ''never'' recover from that experience. '''Euphoria:''' Hey, I was hungry. '''Taggert:''' Aren't we all? We can feast AFTER YOU LISTEN! ''(Another profound silence, likely caused by [[Caius Taggert|Sir Taggert]]'s psychic powers once again.)'' '''Taggert:''' So, it was the most difficult thing, with all these reckless souls flyin' about inside my head. It's why I didn't show meself for 'bout two centuries after I no-clipped into here — I had a constant headache. Once [[Phenomenon 12|the Flicker]] came about, I thought, ''Enough is enough.'' So I performed the most dangerous and taxin' feat I've ever performed in my whole life, even more dangerous than that time down at Drury Lane when one of my fellows, as Tybalt, nearly stabbed me with a real iron sword! Anyways, it was far more dangerous than that. Yes indeed, my friends, I [[Phenomenon 100|split]] [[Level Fun]] in half. ''(An orator's silence follows, though it cannot be said whether from the earlier mental manipulation or the sheer weight of [[Caius Taggert|Taggert]]'s words. It is only broken when Euphoria begins to clap enthusiastically for her master, prompting the other [[The Co-Hosts|Co-Hosts]] to do the same.)'' '''Taggert:''' Yes, yes, I know. I'm awesome. Get through it. '''Hedone:''' It's ''get over it''. '''Taggert:''' Do I look like I care?! ''(sighs)'' Anyways, once that extra piece of [[Level Fun]] was cut off, I directed all those souls into it. And that's how you got '''the Afterparty'''. '''Melody:''' Come on, you spoiled the whole thing! '''Taggert:''' I hate you all. I hate you all so much. '''Euphoria:''' Best frenemies forever! '''Taggert:''' Lord save me. '''> End Log <''' {{Underline|FFFFFF}} ''An eclipse is an astronomical event which occurs when an object is obscured by passing into the shadow of another body or by having another body pass between it and the viewer...'' ''...yet it is still there.'' }}
